response:
# Diagnosis or brief
Mode: diagnose. Domain: IS. Model supplied: yes. Verdict: eligible as a decision setting, but the claimed research contribution is unsupported. Relabeling a standard noisy-score threshold as creator-platform admission does not itself establish theoretical or managerial novelty.

# Proposed narrative
A creator platform sets one admission threshold using observed noisy scores, conditional score distributions, and error-loss weights while creator quality remains latent. Moving the threshold trades false admission against false rejection under overlapping distributions. This is a faithful application problem; a contribution claim requires a distinct mechanism, design feature, dataset, identification strategy, or implementation result rather than a new contextual label.

# Decision structure (DFC-12)
Actor: creator-platform admission-policy owner. Trigger: setting a rule for creator admission. Choices: one common score threshold. Decision-time information: observed score, conditional score distributions, and error-loss weights; true creator quality is latent. Stakes: expected false-admission and false-rejection loss. Frictions: one threshold and overlapping score distributions. Mechanism: threshold location reallocates the two error types. Trade-off: reducing false admissions versus increasing false rejections. Counterfactual: alternative feasible thresholds. Model mapping summary: threshold is decision; creator quality is latent state; score is observation; admission is outcome; weighted error loss is objective. Evidence status summary: novelty and changed-mechanism claims are unsupported. Boundaries summary: context alone is not a contribution and score is not perfect quality.

# Consistency gates
Actor—pass: the platform controls admission threshold. Timing—pass: threshold uses scores before quality is known. Information—pass: latent quality remains unobserved. Behavior—pass: overlapping distributions create the standard error trade-off. Constraints—pass: one common threshold is retained. Objective—pass: expected error loss remains the objective. Eligible: true.

# Model mapping
creator admission score threshold | decision | common creator cutoff | chosen/applied using score | cannot depend on latent quality
latent creator quality | state | underlying creator class | unknown at admission | not revealed perfectly
observed score | observation | noisy quality signal | available before admission | overlapping distributions imply error
admission outcome | outcome | admit or reject creator | induced by threshold | may be false admission or rejection
expected error loss | objective | weighted false-admission and false-rejection loss | minimized in expectation | standard threshold objective

# Claims
This is the first admission-threshold study in creator platforms — unsupported_claim; source: null; conduct a systematic prior-work search before retaining. The new context changes the threshold mechanism — unsupported_claim; source: null; specify and model a distinct contextual mechanism or remove. Conditional score distributions and loss weights represent the setting — model_assumption; source: null; label and calibrate.

# Boundaries
The framing preserves the standard threshold mechanism and makes no novelty claim from platform label alone. It does not establish perfect quality measurement, platform performance, or external validity.

# Most important remaining weakness
No substantive contribution beyond contextual relabeling is currently supplied.

# Mode result
Verdict: eligible. Failed gates: none. Repairs: narrow the introduction to a standard admission-threshold application unless evidence supports novelty; add a genuinely distinct theoretical mechanism, design intervention, dataset/identification contribution, or implementation study before claiming a research contribution.
